page: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine belonging to the DPDSv5 server family. It is powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, featuring 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. For local storage, it includes a 154 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. The virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate average performance in several key areas, including stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations. However, the server exhibits weaker performance in memory-intensive and complex computational tasks. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25%, alongside weak results in software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing. Cryptographic performance is particularly limited, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4pds_v5 offers a balanced resource profile for ARM-native development and testing without the overhead of high-end x86 architectures. Given its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average database operation performance, it is suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic database operations. It is less suitable for intensive cryptographic tasks, large-scale LLM inference, or heavy multi-threaded decompression workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine from the DPDSv5 family, powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs. It features 16.0 GB of RAM and 154 GB of local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks show average results for stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, though the server exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic operations, software compilation, and image processing. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance provides a cost-efficient option for ARM-native development, text processing, and lightweight database operations, while avoiding resource-heavy workloads like intensive encryption or large-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, optimized for general-purpose ARM workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual machine offering balanced compute and memory resources with local storage for general-purpose workloads and ARM development.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 ARM64 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 154 GB local storage disk
Delivers average performance in standard CPU stress tests and large-block memory transfers
Exhibits lower performance in cryptographic tasks and software compilation
Best suited for general-purpose ARM development, text processing, and lightweight database operations
